DECLARATION OF A. JEANNE GROVE IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS TO CROSS-DEFT DANIELLE LAZIER AND DOPPM, INC'S MTN FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT JOHNSON, GREGG JOHNSON, MOLLY

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS SDANIELLE LAZIER AND DPPM, INC.'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. CROSS-DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SET FORTH ANY EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING THAT $7,000 IS WITHIN THE REASONABLE RANGE OF THEIR LIABILITY. "IF THERE IS NO SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT A CRITICAL ASSUMPTION AS TO THE NATURE AND EXTENT OF A SETTLING DEFENDANT'S LIABILITY, THEN A DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH BASED UPON SUCH ASSUMPTION IS AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION." (MATTCO FORGE, INC. V. ARTHUR YOUNG & CO. (1995) 38 CAL.APP.4TH 1337, 1350.) FURTHER, DEFENDANTS HAVE MET THEIR BURDEN UNDER CCP SEC. 877.6(D) OF SHOWING THAT THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT IS NOT REASONABLE, IN LIGHT OF THE FACT THAT DISCLOSURES REGARDING THE DEFECTS ALLEGED IN PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT WERE MADE TO CROSS-DEFENDANTS. (DECLARATION OF A. JEANNE GROVE, EXS. 3-5.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/EG

Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Sep-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS-DEFENDANTS SDANIELLE LAZIER AND DPPM, INC.'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is Denied. Cross-Defendants have not set forth any evidence establishing that $7,000 is within the reasonable range of their liability. "If there is no substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of a settling defendant's liability, then a determination of good faith based upon such assumption is an abuse of discretion." (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350.) Further, Defendants have met their burden under CCP sec. 877.6(d) of showing that the settlement amount is not reasonable, in light of the fact that disclosures regarding the defects alleged in Plaintiff's complaint were made to Cross-Defendants. (Declaration of A. Jeanne Grove, Exs. 3-5.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
